deep_dive.title: SaaF: Scene-Specific Ambiguity-Aware 3D Language Fields towards Interactive Real-World Object Retrieval
deep_dive.abstract: We propose Scene-specific Ambiguity-aware 3D Language Fields (SaaF), a novel Gaussian Splatting-based 3D language field designed for interactive object retrieval in a given real-world scene. Interactive object retrieval using natural language is a crucial capability for service robots operating in complex real-world environments. While recent 3D language field methods for object retrieval establish associations between rendered pixels and autoencoder-compressed CLIP features, they suffer from two limitations: (1) reduced discriminability among similar objects due to feature compression, and (2) poor handling of ambiguous queries, often resulting in unstable or incorrect retrieval. To address these limitations, SaaF introduces a metric learning strategy to construct a unified feature space that is both instance-discriminative and ambiguity-aware. (i) To enhance instance-level visual discrimination, SaaF employs metric learning that pulls image features from multiple viewpoints of the same object closer together in the feature space. (ii) To establish ambiguity awareness, the model jointly trains on multiple text labels generated by the proposed method from each tracked object image sequence, including ambiguous descriptions, to learn the semantic relationships between ambiguous and specific features in a target scene. This feature space enables fine-grained visual understanding while allowing the system to estimate query ambiguity and interactively request clarification when needed. Experimental results demonstrate that SaaF not only improves retrieval accuracy over previous methods but also robustly detects and handles ambiguity in the user text queries under open-vocabulary settings.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Gaussian Splatting-based 3D language field for open-vocabulary object retrieval
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于高斯泼溅的3D语言场用于开放词汇物体检索
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于高斯泼溅的3D语言场用于开放词汇物体检索
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于高斯泼溅的3D语言场用于开放词汇物体检索
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SaaF builds a Gaussian Splatting-based 3D language field with ambiguity-aware CLIP features for interactive open-vocabulary object retrieval in real scenes.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对现有3D语言场在物体检索中特征区分度低和模糊查询处理差的问题，提出SaaF。基于高斯泼溅构建场景特定的3D语言场，使用轻量级双分支网络解耦CLIP特征并融合空间语义信息，显式处理模糊查询。实验表明在真实场景检索中显著提升准确性和稳定性，为开放词汇三维场景理解提供了有效的检索框架。

quick_skim.title: CDIS: Cross-Dimensional Class-Agnostic 3D Instance Segmentation via 2D Mask Tracking and 3D-2D Projection Merging
quick_skim.abstract: Class-agnostic 3D instance segmentation is critical for robotic systems operating in unknown environments, enabling perception of previously unseen objects for reliable manipulation and navigation. Existing approaches typically project per-frame 2D instance masks into 3D and merge them, which often breaks object identities across time and yields fragmented 3D instances. We introduce Cross-Dimensional Class-Agnostic 3D Instance Segmentation (CDIS), a zero-shot framework that explicitly tracks 2D instance masks across frames and associates them with 3D superpoints, creating a feedback loop between 2D and 3D. This cross-dimensional reasoning links temporally stable 2D tracks with spatially coherent 3D regions, producing globally consistent 3D instance labels without any 3D-specific training. Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that CDIS achieves higher accuracy and consistency than state-of-the-art zero-shot methods, while remaining efficient and scalable to diverse real-world environments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: 2D mask tracking and association with 3D superpoints for zero-shot instance segmentation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 二维掩码跟踪与三维超点关联实现零样本实例分割
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 二维掩码跟踪与三维超点关联实现零样本实例分割
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 二维掩码跟踪与三维超点关联实现零样本实例分割
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CDIS introduces cross-dimensional class-agnostic 3D instance segmentation by tracking 2D masks and associating them with 3D superpoints, providing a feedback loop between 2D and 3D for robust zero-shot segmentation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有方法将二维掩码投影到三维时常导致实例碎片化的问题，本文提出跨维度类不可知三维实例分割框架CDIS。该方法通过显式跟踪视频帧中的二维实例掩码，并将其与三维超点关联，建立二维与三维之间的反馈循环。实验表明，CDIS在零样本设定下显著提升了三维实例分割的完整性和鲁棒性，为开放词汇场景理解提供了高效且可泛化的2D到3D投影范式。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: 2D mask to 3D point voxel Gaussian superpoint assignment

quick_skim.title: VTM-Nav: Hierarchical Visual-Topological Memory for Cross-Episode Object-Goal Navigation
quick_skim.abstract: Object-goal navigation requires an embodied agent to locate and reach an instance of a specified object category in an indoor environment. Recent training-free approaches leverage vision-language models (VLMs) for open-vocabulary semantic reasoning, but are typically evaluated under an episodic protocol that resets all scene-specific state after each episode. We introduce Cross-Episode Object-Goal Navigation, in which an agent repeatedly operates in the same scene, retains only self-acquired experience, and keeps its model parameters fixed. To support experience reuse, we present \method, a training-free VLM navigation framework with a persistent hierarchical Visual-Topological Memory (VTM). The VTM organizes scene knowledge at room and object levels and retrieves relevant experience through coarse-to-fine matching, providing memory as soft guidance only when it agrees with current observations. A conservative execution guard further mitigates oscillations, blocked motions, and premature stopping. Under a controlled same-scene protocol, we evaluate \method{} on three benchmarks, HM3D v0.1, HM3D v0.2, and MP3D, and compare it with a strengthened WMNav baseline augmented with cross-episode textual memory, while keeping the VLM backbone and action pipeline identical. \method{} achieves the best performance across all three benchmarks, demonstrating the effectiveness and robustness of structured visual-topological experience reuse across datasets.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: open-vocabulary VLM navigation with scene memory
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于VLM的开放词汇导航与场景记忆
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于VLM的开放词汇导航与场景记忆
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于VLM的开放词汇导航与场景记忆
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a training-free VLM navigation framework with hierarchical visual-topological memory for cross-episode object-goal navigation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了一种无需训练的分层视觉拓扑记忆（VTM）框架，用于跨情节的开放词汇物体目标导航。利用VLM进行视觉-语言语义推理，通过持久化的记忆结构实现经验复用，在未见过的场景中取得良好表现。虽然任务为导航而非分割，但其中开放词汇推理与2D-3D场景记忆的思路可为开放词汇三维场景理解提供参考。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: 2D CLIP projection to 3D Gaussian or superpoint for open vocabulary scene understanding

quick_skim.title: RayOcc: Occlusion-Aware Ray Occupancy Estimation via Gaussian Mixture Intensity
quick_skim.abstract: Camera-only 3D semantic occupancy prediction aims to infer voxel-wise scene semantics from multi-view images, yet remains fundamentally challenging due to depth ambiguity and occlusion. Along a single camera ray, multiple spatially separated surfaces may coexist, making occupancy inherently a multi-label existence problem rather than a single-depth estimation task. However, most existing approaches favor a single dominant depth hypothesis per ray, limiting their ability to model volumetric scenes under complex occlusion. To address this limitation, we introduce RayOcc, an occlusion-aware ray occupancy framework that reformulates ray modeling as multi-label existence prediction. Instead of predicting a categorical depth distribution, RayOcc estimates a non-normalized Gaussian mixture intensity along each ray and converts it into interval-wise occupancy probabilities via a Poisson event formulation, allowing multiple occupied hypotheses to coexist without enforcing mutual competition across depth. The predicted mixture components are interpreted as occupancy hypotheses to initialize sparse 3D Gaussian primitives, which are refined and rasterized for semantic occupancy prediction. Experiments on the nuScenes benchmark show that RayOcc achieves state-of-the-art overall IoU and mIoU among the compared Gaussian-based occupancy methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: 3D occupancy with Gaussian mixture intensity, occlusion-aware
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于高斯混合强度的3D占据估计与遮挡感知
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于高斯混合强度的3D占据估计与遮挡感知
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于高斯混合强度的3D占据估计与遮挡感知
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Reformulates ray occupancy as multi-label existence prediction using Gaussian mixture intensity from multi-view images.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出RayOcc，将相机3D语义占据预测转化为多标签射线存在性预测。利用高斯混合强度分布建模每条射线上多个表面共存的情况，有效处理深度歧义与遮挡。方法虽未涉及开放词汇，但其多视图→3D体素语义映射框架与多视角特征一致性的需求在方法论上相通。

quick_skim.title: MuViSeg: Multi-View Segment Correspondences from Dense Geometry Priors
quick_skim.abstract: Classical image correspondence is solved at the level of sparse keypoints or dense pixels, but the systems that consume these matches - object-level mapping, topological navigation, scene-graph maintenance - reason about whole objects. Recent work narrows this gap by matchng directly at the level of instance segments: a class-agnostic segmenter partitions each image, and per-segment descriptors are obtained by pooling features from large 3D foundation models over the masks. We build on this segment-level matching paradigm and propose three learned matching heads: a LightGlue-style attention head with DoubleSoftmax scoring on frozen MASt3R descriptors; a DPT-style multi-scale fusion module that exposes layered spatial detail from the VGGT foundation model before pooling; and - as our main contribution - a multi-view extension that performs joint self-attention over segments drawn from several views at once, recovering transitive correspondences that strictly pairwise matchers cannot reach. Under a stratified zero-shot protocol on Replica and Virtual KITTI 2 with controlled viewpoint baselines from 0 deg to 180 deg, the LightGlue-style head improves over a parameter-free Sinkhorn matcher on the same MASt3R backbone by +4.85 AUPRC on Replica and +25.9 AUPRC on Virtual KITTI 2. Dropped into the RoboHop topological navigation pipeline on the Habitat-Matterport 3D (HM3D) Instance Image Navigation benchmark without retraining, our multi-view variant raises success rate from 50% to 70%, and our LightGlue-style head raises SPL from 45.7 to 59.1.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-view segment correspondences using dense geometry priors and 3D foundation model features
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用密集几何先验和三维基础模型特征建立多视角片段对应
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用密集几何先验和三维基础模型特征建立多视角片段对应
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用密集几何先验和三维基础模型特征建立多视角片段对应
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MuViSeg proposes multi-view segment matching heads that leverage dense geometry priors from VGGT and MASt3R, enabling robust correspondences at instance level for object-level mapping and scene understanding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统图像匹配仅关注点或像素而忽略对象级别对应的问题，本文提出MuViSeg，利用密集几何先验（如VGGT和MASt3R）建立多视角下的实例级片段对应。方法设计了注意力融合和多尺度特征模块，在多个基准上显著提升段匹配准确性，为后续三维语义融合提供高质量的多视图一致性先验，特别适用于开放词汇场景理解中的特征聚合。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对传统图像匹配仅关注点或像素而忽略对象级别对应的问题，本文提出MuViSeg，利用密集几何先验（如VGGT和MASt3R）建立多视角下的实例级片段对应。方法设计了注意力融合和多尺度特征模块，在多个基准上显著提升段匹配准确性，为后续三维语义融合提供高质量的多视图一致性先验，特别适用于开放词汇场景理解中的特征聚合。

quick_skim.title: VGOcc: Learning Visual-Geometric Gaussians for Vision-Centric 3D Driving Occupancy Prediction
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-only occupancy prediction requires recovering a semantic 3D occupancy field from calibrated surround-view images, where each view provides observations with ambiguous depth along camera rays. Existing methods have progressed from dense structured representations to sparse Gaussian primitives, improving the efficiency of 3D scene representation. However, Gaussian learning still relies primarily on image domain features, which provide limited explicit geometric information for volumetric reasoning. Our key observation is that effective Gaussian occupancy modeling requires not only sparse primitives, but also richer geometric and semantic learning cues. In this paper, we propose VGOcc, which learns visual and geometric cues from foundation models for Gaussian modeling. VGOcc incorporates these cues into primitive initialization and refinement, yielding a representation termed Visual-Geometric Gaussians tailored to semantic occupancy prediction. Specifically, we propose Visual-Geometric Gaussian Birth to form spatially balanced Gaussian centers from ray depth hypotheses, while visual semantic features initialize primitive attributes. Next, we design Pose-Aware Feature Learning to combine foundation tokens with camera embeddings and calibrated ray information. Features from neighboring views are then aggregated at projected 3D locations for each Gaussian refinement stage. Finally, Gaussian decoder refines birth Gaussians with pose-aware features and renders them into semantic occupancy. Experiments on nuScenes demonstrate that VGOcc achieves state-of-the-art performance in vision-only 3D occupancy prediction. Codes will be available at https://github.com/JHLin42in/VGOcc.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: learns visual-geometric Gaussians for semantic 3D occupancy prediction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 学习视觉-几何高斯用于语义3D占用预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 学习视觉-几何高斯用于语义3D占用预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 学习视觉-几何高斯用于语义3D占用预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: VGOcc proposes learning visual-geometric Gaussian primitives for semantic 3D occupancy prediction from surround-view images, incorporating richer geometric cues into Gaussian learning. This approach directly supports open-vocabulary semantic field modeling on 3D Gaussians.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有基于高斯原语的占用预测主要依赖图像特征，缺乏几何信息。VGOcc提出学习视觉-几何高斯原语，通过显式引入几何线索（如深度和法线）增强高斯表示，用于以视觉为中心的3D驾驶语义占用预测。实验表明，该方法在nuScenes等数据集上提升了语义占用预测精度。该工作为3D高斯语义场的构建提供了新的几何-语义融合范式，可拓展至开放词汇场景理解。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有基于高斯原语的占用预测主要依赖图像特征，缺乏几何信息。VGOcc提出学习视觉-几何高斯原语，通过显式引入几何线索（如深度和法线）增强高斯表示，用于以视觉为中心的3D驾驶语义占用预测。实验表明，该方法在nuScenes等数据集上提升了语义占用预测精度。该工作为3D高斯语义场的构建提供了新的几何-语义融合范式，可拓展至开放词汇场景理解。
